title: Cal_FixDown_LensDist
description:
Cal_FixDown_LensDist

Calibration Procedure: Fixed Downward (when performing correction of lens distortion and camera tilt)

This calibration will allow you to search for objects from a fixed camera looking down into the robot work envelope and get their positions in the robot world.

Step 1: Mount the camera

(1) Mount the camera so that it will be looking down into the robot envelope.  Make sure that the robot will clear the camera.

Step 2: Make a dot grid pattern

(1) Create a dot grid pattern which has more than 100 points in the field of view of the camera.  The grid pattern must cover the entire field of view with minimum distortion.  Accuracy of the grid pattern affects accuracy of image processing.

Step 3: Make a calibration plate

(1) Make a plate with nine holes or targets that span the field of view of the camera.

Step 4: Create vision sequence to find dot grid pattern

(1) Set the dot grid pattern.

(2) Create a sequence to find the dot grid pattern.

(3) Select the Lamp property, if required.

(4) Create a Blob object and expand the search area to the full screen.

(5) Set the NumberToFind property to All.

(6) Set the RejectOnEdge property to True.

(7) Set the ThresholdAuto, ThresholdHigh, ThresholdLow, MinArea, and MaxArea prperties, if required.

(8) After creating the sequence, check if it finds more than 100 points.

Example of the image of dot grid pattern by the camera with tilt angle

Step 5: Create vision sequence to find calibration reference targets

(1) Create a sequence to locate the calibration targets.  You can create one object for each target, or you can create one object to find all nine targets, provided that the object can return multiple results (e.g. Blob, Correlation, or Geometric objects).

(2) After creating the sequence, check if it finds all nine points.

Step 6: Create a calibration scheme

(1) Click the New Calibration button on the Vision Guide 7.0 tool bar or select New Calibration from the Vision menu.  Enter the name for the new scheme.

Step 7: Setting for correction of lens distortion and camera tilt

(1) Set the Enable property below the DistCorrect property to True.

(2) Select the sequence to find the dot grid pattern for the TargetSeq property below the DistCorrect property.

(3) Select the Cal property below the DistCorrect property and execute detection of the dot grid pattern.

(4) By selecting the calibration scheme created in Step 6 for the Calibration property of the sequence for locating the calibration targets, you can check the image which lens distortion and camera tilt are corrected.  Distortion is corrected automatically when calibration is executed even if this step is skipped.

Example of the image of dot grid pattern after correcting lens distortion and camera tilt

Step 8: Teach points

(1) Remove the dot grid pattern, and then set the calibration plate.

(2) Set the TargetSequence property.

(3) Set the Lamp property, if required.

(4) Set the RobotArm, RobotLocal, RobotNumber, and RobotTool properties.

(5) On Vision Guide Calibration tab, click the <Teach Points> button.  This will open the [Teach Calibration Points] window.

(6) Follow the instructions in the message box near the bottom of the dialog to teach each reference point.  You will be prompted to teach the point and if TwoRefPoints is true, you must move the U axis 180 degrees to teach the point again.  Click on the <Next> button to move to the next step.

Step 9: Calibrate

(1) On Vision Guide Calibration tab, click the Calibrate button to start the calibration cycle.  The calibration software will locate the nine targets and then locate them again to verify calibration and collect statistical data.  You can click the Abort button to stop the calibration cycle.
